pi_items.source: MozillaZine Knowledge Base (Network.dns.disablePrefetch); Firefox security hardening documentation. Default value is false; setting to true disables DNS prefetching for privacy.
Mozilla Support documentation (How to stop Firefox from making automatic connections); Firefox prefetching documentation. Default value is true; setting to false disables link prefetching.
Mozilla Support forum (Where can I disable WebRTC and PeerConnection?) and CIS Mozilla Firefox Benchmark. Setting to false disables WebRTC to prevent IP address leaks.
Mozilla Support forum (Using 'geo.enabled' to configure geolocation) and Firefox/Privacy ArchWiki. Setting to false disables geolocation requests from websites.
Mozilla Support (Resist Fingerprinting article); Firefox security hardening guides. Setting to true standardizes browser behavior to resist browser fingerprinting attacks.
Mozilla Support forum (ERROR:Network.http.sendRefererHeader = 0); CIS Mozilla Firefox Benchmark. Value 0 means 'never send Referer header', disabling referrer tracking.
Mozilla Bugzilla (Bug 1546198); Bleeping Computer article on Mozilla Firefox hyperlink ping tracking. Default is false; disables hyperlink auditing/ping tracking.
Mozilla Support (How to unblock WebGL); techdows.com guide on disabling WebGL in Firefox. Setting to true disables WebGL for security.
Mozilla Support (Enhanced Tracking Protection in Firefox for desktop). Setting to 'strict' enables Strict Enhanced Tracking Protection which blocks all cross-site cookies and tracking content.
Mozilla's official MDN Web Docs website (developer.mozilla.org); Mozilla Support documentation on setting homepage. Valid public URL for developer documentation.
Environment setup scripts (setup_firefox.sh) create /home/ga/Documents directory. File path follows standard Linux home directory conventions.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires configuring Firefox browser security settings according to industry-standard hardening practices. All 8 about:config preferences (network.dns.disablePrefetch=true, network.prefetch-next=false, media.peerconnection.enabled=false, geo.enabled=false, privacy.resistFingerprinting=true, network.http.sendRefererHeader=0, browser.send_pings=false, webgl.disabled=true) are real Firefox preferences with documented security benefits. The Enhanced Tracking Protection setting (browser.contentblocking.category=strict) is a standard Firefox security control. The homepage is set to https://developer.mozilla.org, which is Mozilla's official MDN Web Docs website. The task outputs results to /home/ga/Documents/browser_security_config.json. All metadata claims refer to actual Firefox preferences and legitimate security hardening measures documented by Mozilla and security benchmarks (CIS).
